title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 21, 2026 - Market Rebounds Amid Strong Earnings, Geopolitical Concerns

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Climb as Earnings Season Takes Center Stage, Geopolitical Tensions Loom
items.summary: U.S. stock markets rebounded today, driven by a strong showing in the technology and semiconductor sectors and a wave of positive corporate earnings reports. This positive momentum helped snap a three-day losing streak for major indices. However, overall market sentiment remains cautious as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including ongoing U.S. strikes against Iran and threats of a Red Sea blockade, continue to fuel volatility and keep oil prices elevated. Investors are closely monitoring these developments alongside upcoming earnings from major tech companies for signals on AI spending and the economic outlook.

items.headline: Coinbase Global Surges on Crypto Rally
items.summary: Coinbase Global surged amidst a broader cryptocurrency market rally, optimism around the U.S. CLARITY Act, and Bitcoin reclaiming the $65,000 threshold.

items.headline: Teradyne Rises on Tech Recovery
items.summary: Teradyne saw significant gains driven by a broader technology sector recovery and positive sentiment in anticipation of its upcoming second-quarter earnings report.

items.headline: Western Digital Climbs on Semiconductor Strength
items.summary: Western Digital's stock climbed on the back of strength across memory and semiconductor stocks, analyst price target increases, and robust long-term demand for AI storage solutions.

items.headline: 3M Beats Q2 Expectations, Raises Guidance
items.summary: 3M reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding expectations and increasing its full-year adjusted EPS guidance, leading to a significant stock increase.

items.headline: Danaher Falls Midday Despite Strong Q2 Earnings
items.summary: Despite reporting better-than-expected second-quarter results with net earnings up 60% year-over-year, Danaher was noted as a top midday loser, declining significantly.

items.headline: Zhengye Biotechnology Experiences Steep Decline
items.summary: Zhengye Biotechnology Holding saw a substantial decline in its stock price today.

items.headline: MV Oil Trust Down Following Distribution Announcement
items.summary: MV Oil Trust recorded a significant loss after announcing its final trust distribution.

items.headline: Zions Bancorp Drops in Premarket Trading
items.summary: Zions Bancorp experienced a decline in premarket trading.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Heats Up with Major Beats and Future Outlooks
items.summary: Second-quarter earnings reports are driving market movements, with General Motors ($GM) and 3M ($MMM) both surpassing Wall Street's expectations. Danaher ($DHR) also reported strong results, with net earnings up 60% year-over-year. Investors are now keenly awaiting reports from tech giants like Alphabet, IBM, and Tesla for insights into artificial intelligence spending and their outlooks for the second half of the year. Halliburton ($HAL), Charles Schwab ($SCHW), and Capital One Financial ($COF) are among other major companies scheduled to report today.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Escalate, Keeping Oil Prices High and Market Cautious
items.summary: The ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and U.S. military strikes against Iran, coupled with threats of a Red Sea blockade by Houthis, continue to weigh on global markets. Rising oil prices are a direct consequence of these tensions, adding to inflation concerns. This geopolitical backdrop is contributing to a cautious market sentiment despite strong corporate earnings.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data: Inflation Cools While Producer Prices Rise
items.summary: Last week's June Consumer Price Index (CPI) showed a 0.4% decline, marking the first decrease since 2020, which could alleviate fears of rising interest rates. However, the Producer Price Index (PPI) for final demand increased by 5.5% over the 12 months ending June 2026, with energy prices seeing a significant 23.0% increase. Retail sales in June rose 0.2% month-over-month, with online retail sales surging 1.9%, indicating solid consumer spending.

items.headline: Tech and Semiconductors Lead Market Rebound; Healthcare and Industrials Lag
items.summary: The technology and semiconductor sectors were the primary drivers of today's market rebound, with strong investor interest in AI-related stocks. Conversely, healthcare and industrial stocks faced headwinds and contributed to Monday's market decline, with some weakness potentially carrying over into Tuesday's trading. Cryptocurrencies also saw a recovery, with Bitcoin reclaiming the $65,000 level.
